profile.name: quick-experiment
profile.intent: Run lightweight scratchpad experiments — launch a job, watch it, debug crashes, estimate cost — without committing to the full ML research paper lifecycle (no rebuttal / submission / camera-ready / paper-writing apparatus).

routing.handoff_policy: Enter through experiment-evidence-router, then hand off to run-experiment, experiment-debugger, compute-budget-planner, or result-diagnosis according to lifecycle stage. Depends on core-ops for git/memory/docs/sidecar substrate and on automation for remote-runtime and scheduler-probe skills; do not duplicate those here.

install_policy.notes: Install alongside core-ops + automation when a folder runs experiments without producing a paper. Common targets: idea-validation scratchpad, baseline reproduction, ad-hoc debugging session, sweep without ablation-rigor commitment. Do not auto-install paper-writing or submission skills.

workflow_contract.lane_selection_rule: Use combined for durable experiment turns because submission should follow design and result interpretation; use action for bounded job submission, evidence for status / debugging audits, and no-contract only for transient read-only probes.

required_preflight: Detect compute budget envelope and resource availability before submitting any non-trivial job.
Size a smoke test (<30 min) before any formal run.
Classify whether the action is exploratory scratchpad (no paper claim attached) or a step toward a real claim — escalate to ml-research if the latter.
forbidden_actions: Do not promote scratchpad results into paper-facing tables, figures, or claim boards without an explicit lifecycle transition.
Do not run formal multi-seed sweeps without an experiment-design-planner step first.
Do not write rebuttal, camera-ready, or submission artifacts from a quick-experiment folder; that requires the full ml-research bundle.
action_lane.approval_boundary: Escalate before opening a paper writing contract, before committing scratchpad outputs to a public release, or before a sweep exceeds the declared compute budget.

evidence_lane.confidence_policy: Treat scratchpad results as exploratory until variance and confounds are addressed; do not phrase one-seed runs as durable claims.
evidence_lane.citation_policy: Cite job IDs, status artifact paths, and metric tables; do not import scratchpad findings as paper claims without a separate ml-research lifecycle step.

combined_lane.handoff_artifact: status artifact under docs/ops/runs/, scratchpad result report, or compute budget record
combined_lane.staleness_trigger: Job state changes, new submission, exhausted compute budget, or transition to a paper-bound experiment.

no_contract_lane.allowed_when: The probe is read-only, transient, does not submit a job, does not write status artifacts, and does not modify remote scheduler state.
no_contract_lane.escalation_trigger: The task requires job submission, scheduler mutation, credential access, durable result interpretation, or transition into paper lifecycle.

acceptance_checks: Kernel example references the quick-experiment profile and experiment-evidence-router entrypoint.
Action lane names scheduler / remote / package-env mutation surfaces with an explicit escalation rule for paper-bound work.
No paper-writing, submission, camera-ready, or rebuttal skills are required by default; depends on core-ops and automation for shared substrate.

privacy_boundary.publication_audit_required_when: Use memory-publication-auditor before turning scratchpad results, raw job logs, or internal sweep configurations into public quick-experiment rules or paper-facing claims.

selection_contexts: Select quick-experiment directly for scratchpad / idea-validation / baseline-reproduction folders without paper-writing context.
Pair quick-experiment with core-ops and automation; the three together cover the lightweight-experiment work without pulling in the full ml-research bundle.
Do not require quick-experiment to beat ml-research in a full ML research project; ml-research absorbs quick-experiment skills there and remains the right entrypoint.
selection_semantics.delegation_policy: After selection, delegate to experiment-evidence-router, then to run-experiment, experiment-debugger, compute-budget-planner, or result-diagnosis. Depend on core-ops and automation rather than reimplementing git / memory / docs / remote / status-probe behavior.
selection_semantics.installable_manifest_gate: Installable manifests should preserve scratchpad-experiment selection before leaf-skill routing and keep paper-writing skills out of the default route.

promotion_gates: Scratchpad experiment workflows can run without pulling paper-writing or submission skills.
Lifecycle boundary between quick-experiment and ml-research is explicit: scratchpad results require an explicit transition before becoming paper claims.
Runtime adapters can be generated without duplicating source logic into Codex or Claude Code metadata.
Depends-on relationships (core-ops + automation) are documented and respected by installers and routers.
rejection_checks: Do not extract if quick-experiment still requires paper-writing or rebuttal skills by default.
Do not make credentials, raw job logs, or local cluster paths part of public kernel examples.
Do not split before the depends-on chain to core-ops and automation is recorded in profile-index and adapter docs.
